near_leaf_pairs.note: Same call: the green 'Equities' dial in Main Asset Classes (snapshot lines 45-48). Model mapped it to the Asset Allocation - Broad leaf, GT to the Equities - Geo leaf 'Global Equities'. Pure leaf-name drift vs pilot-04, which evidently matched GT's choice.

Same call: the red/down 'Emerging markets USD' dial under Investment grade credit (snapshot lines 120-123); both commentaries quote the same downgrade text. Model's leaf (EM IG Credit) is arguably the more faithful mapping since the dial sits in the IG credit section; GT mapped it to sovereign hard-currency debt.

model_only_judgments.reason: Prose 'remain underweight bonds as the level of yields poses a valuation risk to equities' is verified (snapshot line 37), but this adds a third, broadest level on top of the Government bonds main dial and the regional sub-dials; GT keeps only the sub-dial level for bonds.

Derived from the yield forecast 'expect the US 10 year to rise to 4.5%' (snapshot line 20). No intermediate-maturity dial exists in the source, and the call duplicates the US Treasuries red dial already exact-matched at U; converting a point yield forecast into a second Treasury leaf is inference, not a stated view.

Source says 'close the overweight to international value stocks' (snapshot lines 25-26). Closing an overweight implies moving to neutral, not going underweight; the U view over-reads the direction, and there is no value-factor dial in the grid.

The red 'Government bonds' main-asset-class dial is verified (snapshot lines 49-53). Model emits the main dial alongside the regional sub-dials; GT keeps only the sub-dials for government bonds (though it did keep main dials for Equities and Commodities).
The red/down 'Corporate bonds (credit)' main dial is verified (snapshot lines 58-60). Model emits the main-level credit dial while GT keeps only the IG/HY sub-dials.

Yellow/down 'Energy' dial with take-profit text verified (snapshot lines 132-136). GT includes the sibling commodity sub-dials Gold and Agriculture but omitted Energy; the model row is a correct capture of a real dial.
Yellow 'Industrial metals' dial verified (snapshot lines 141-145) and Copper/Industrial is the taxonomy's industrial-metals leaf. GT omitted it despite keeping other commodity sub-dials.

notes: All 26 exact matches agree on view and both gt_only rows resolve as near-leaf pairs with agreeing views, so pilot-05's two 'misses' are leaf-name drift (Equities - General vs Global Equities; EM IG Credit vs EM Debt - Hard Currency), not extraction failures. GT's level convention is internally inconsistent: it keeps main dials for Equities and Commodities but drops the Government bonds and Corporate bonds mains, and keeps Gold/Agriculture while dropping the Energy and Industrial metals sub-dials. The only genuine quality issues on the model side are two inference rows (Intermediate US Treasuries from a yield forecast; Global ex-US Value mapping a closed overweight to U instead of N).
